page.title: Thyroid Cancer (Well-Differentiated) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Thyroid Cancer: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Thyroid Cancer: 189 alterations and 321 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: A high level of circulating 25(OH)D was associated with a decreased thyroid cancer risk.
Assessment of five typical environmental endocrine disruptors and thyroid cancer risk.
Radioiodine-131 based ablation strategy for patients with thyroid cancer has a failure rate of 52.4%.
Age of diagnosing physician impacts the incidence of thyroid cancer in a population.
Beta-carotene, vitamin C and vitamin E deficiencies may play a role in the pathogenesis of thyroid cancer.
Dental x-rays, particularly multiple exposures, may be associated with an increased risk of thyroid cancer.
Drinking herbal teas, especially chamomile over 30 years may significantly reduced the risk of thyroid cancer and benign thyroid diseases development by almost 80%.
Exposure to bisphenol AF and diethylhexylphthalate increases susceptibility to develop differentiated thyroid cancer in patients with thyroid nodules.
Genetic susceptibility may modify the association between cell phone use and thyroid cancer.
Higher intake of whole grain food is associated with thyroid cancer.
Increase in thyroid cancer incidence cannot be attributed to better diagnostic procedures and the role of both ionizing and non-ionizing radiation should be further studied.
Iodine deficiency is associated with increased risk fo thyroid cancer.
Nodule size does not appear to be an independent predictor of thyroid malignancy.
Oxidative stress mediates the associations between phthalate exposures and thyroid cancer/benign nodule risk.
Salivary gland toxicity after radioiodine therapy for thyroid cancer is common.
Selenium deficiency may contribute to the pathogenesis of thyroid cancer.
Starchy foods, cheese, butter and oils other than olive increase the risk for thyroid cancer, whereas fruits and vegetable consumption lowers the risk.
Statin use is associated with thyroid cancer in female patients

The positive clinical therapeutically effects of Escin on advanced thyroid cancer.
There is increased lipid peroxidation and impaired enzymatic antioxidant defense mechanism in thyroid tissue with multinodular goiter and papillary carcinoma.
These results found an elevated risk of thyroid microcarcinoma associated with long-term and more frequent use of cell phones.
These results indicate that incidental diagnosis of thyroid nodules is a significant contributor to thyroid cancer diagnosis.
These results suggest that coffee drinking may protect against liver, ovarian, thyroid and endometrial cancers and melanoma.
These results suggest the efficacy of aromatherapy in the prevention of treatment-related salivary gland disorder.
Vitamin E showed a protective effect on parotid excretion function in patients with differentiated thyroid carcinoma who underwent 131I treatment.
Wheat germ agglutinin (WGA) and concanavalin A bind to benign and malignant thyroid nodules.
Zinc deficiency may contribute to the pathogenesis of thyroid cancer.
BPA influences thyroid cancer proliferatio.
Honokiol has a unique application in chemotherapy for human thyroid cancers.
Mandarin juice exerts in vitro antiproliferative effects on anaplastic thyroid carcinoma cells, associated with a reduction of migration.
There appears to be a protective role of microchimerism in thyroid cancer.
A case report of Non-Hodgkin's lymphoma of the thyroid after many years of immunizations.

Spontaneous regression of metastatic papillary thyroid cancer in a lymph node.
Spontaneous regression of primary thyroid lymphoma.

BPA could enhance the susceptibility to Thyroid carcinoma stimulated by DHPN and iodine excess.

natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Beta-carotene, vitamin C and vitamin E deficiencies may play a role in the pathogenesis of thyroid cancer.

natural_products.name: Fruit: All
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Starchy foods, cheese, butter and oils other than olive increase the risk for thyroid cancer, whereas fruits and vegetable consumption lowers the r

natural_products.name: Vegetables: All
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Starchy foods, cheese, butter and oils other than olive increase the risk for thyroid cancer, whereas fruits and vegetable consumption lowers the r

natural_products.name: Horse Chestnut
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: The positive clinical therapeutically effects of Escin on advanced thyroid cancer.

natural_products.name: Coffee
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: These results suggest that coffee drinking may protect against liver, ovarian, thyroid and endometrial cancers and melanoma.

natural_products.name: Essential Oils: Unspecified
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: These results suggest the efficacy of aromatherapy in the prevention of treatment-related salivary gland disorder.

natural_products.name: Orange: Mandarin
natural_products.key_findings: Human In Vitro: Mandarin juice exerts in vitro antiproliferative effects on anaplastic thyroid carcinoma cells, associated with a reduction of migration.

natural_products.name: Bupleurum
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study, In Vitro Study: Antitumor mechanism of Saikosaponin A in the Xiaoying Sanjie Decoction for treatment of anaplastic thyroid cancer.

natural_products.name: Broccoli Sprouts
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study, In Vitro Study: Broccoli sprouts and their influence on thyroid function.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Not applicable — surgical resection is standard; papillary microcarcinoma (<1 cm, low-risk): active surveillance achieves 99.8%+ cancer-specific survival at 10 years without immediate surgery in Japanese cohorts
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Total thyroidectomy + RAI for intermediate/high-risk: 90-95% disease-free survival at 10 years; active surveillance for low-risk papillary microcarcinoma: non-inferior to surgery in multiple cohorts; lenvatinib + pembrolizumab for RAI-refractory: OS benefit (LEAP-011 ongoing); vemurafenib for BRAF V600E mutant: response in 38%
remission.gap_size: Moderate — overtreatment of low-risk disease is the dominant problem (too many total thyroidectomies in low-risk disease)
remission.barrier_detail: The Kuma Hospital and Cancer Institute Hospital experience in Japan showing non-inferiority of active surveillance for low-risk papillary microcarcinoma has not been widely adopted outside Japan; total thyroidectomy with radioiodine for all differentiated thyroid cancer remains common despite lack of benefit in low-risk disease
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — ATA task force expanded active surveillance indications; lenvatinib now standard second-line; 2018 ATA guidelines reduced RAI use in low-risk; total thyroidectomy + RAI standard since 1990s (largely unchanged)
remission.outdated_treatments: Total thyroidectomy + RAI for all papillary thyroid microcarcinomas (overtreatment; active surveillance non-inferior in low-risk); TSH suppression to very low levels in low-risk patients (increases cardiovascular and bone risk without cancer benefit); routine post-thyroidectomy calcium supplementation without individualised assessment
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Active surveillance for low-risk PTMC (expanding from Japan to US/Europe); selenium supplementation for Hashimoto's co-existing with differentiated thyroid cancer (reduces anti-TPO, may improve treatment tolerance); exercise during thyroid cancer treatment
remission.frontier_signal: BRAF/MEK inhibitors for advanced BRAF V600E papillary thyroid cancer (dabrafenib + trametinib — FDA approved 2018 for other cancers, compassionate use thyroid cancer); RET inhibitors (selpercatinib, pralsetinib) for RET-mutant medullary thyroid cancer; NTRK inhibitors (larotrectinib) for NTRK-fusion thyroid cancers
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
